actions.description: Continued transformation from a listed investment holding company into a third-party alternative asset manager, growing third-party AUM from approximately EUR 22B (2022) to EUR 27.5B (H1 2025) while maintaining a significant proprietary balance sheet of approximately EUR 9.3B.
actions.stated_rationale: Management described the transformation as necessary to unlock the fee-related revenue stream and valuation re-rating that pure-play alt managers command, while retaining the balance sheet as a co-investment advantage and alignment mechanism.
actions.observable_metric_impact: Third-party AUM grew from EUR 22B to EUR 27.5B (+10% YoY at H1 2025). FRE from third-party management fees became an increasingly material contributor to total earnings. Balance sheet NAV appreciated through direct investment returns.
actions.op_prereq_summary: Required buildout of institutional distribution and investor relations capabilities, development of third-party fund structures alongside proprietary balance-sheet investments, and LP education on Eurazeo's hybrid model.

actions.description: Expanded private debt platform through multiple fund vintages and co-lending arrangements, growing debt AUM to approximately EUR 6B by H1 2025 as the fastest-growing segment of the third-party platform.
actions.stated_rationale: Management identified private debt as an attractive adjacent strategy leveraging Eurazeo's mid-market PE deal flow and origination relationships to source credit transactions at favorable terms.
actions.observable_metric_impact: Private debt AUM reached approximately EUR 6B. Multiple direct lending fund vintages raised. Debt strategies provided fee revenue diversification beyond traditional PE management fees and carried interest.
actions.op_prereq_summary: Required dedicated credit origination and underwriting team, separate fund structures from PE vehicles, and regulatory compliance for lending activities across European jurisdictions.

actions.description: Launched dedicated real estate and infrastructure fund strategies targeting the European mid-market, diversifying Eurazeo's third-party platform beyond its core PE and growth equity franchises.
actions.stated_rationale: Management described real assets as a natural extension of Eurazeo's European mid-market expertise, providing LPs with a diversified allocation solution across private equity, debt, and real assets from a single European manager.
actions.observable_metric_impact: Real assets strategies contributed to overall third-party AUM growth. Total AUM reached EUR 36.8B at H1 2025 (+4% YoY). Product diversification reduced concentration risk in PE flagship fundraising cycles.
actions.op_prereq_summary: Required dedicated real estate and infrastructure investment teams, sector-specific fund structures, and development of LP relationships beyond Eurazeo's traditional PE investor base.

actions.description: Raised Antin Infrastructure Partners Fund V (Flagship V) targeting EUR 10B+, the firm's largest flagship fund to date, focused on mid-cap and large-cap European infrastructure investments across energy transition, digital, transportation, and social infrastructure.
actions.stated_rationale: Management positioned Fund V as a continuation of Antin's pure-play infrastructure model, targeting the growing LP allocation to infrastructure driven by energy transition, digitalization, and government stimulus programs across Europe.
actions.observable_metric_impact: Flagship V fundraising in progress through 2025-2026. Prior flagship (Fund IV) raised EUR 6.5B. Fee-paying AUM of EUR 21.8B (+6.2% YoY). Total AUM reached EUR 33B+ by H1 2025.
actions.op_prereq_summary: Required global LP roadshow across institutional investors, demonstrated track record from Fund III and Fund IV vintages, and EU SFDR Article 8 fund classification and sustainability reporting infrastructure.

actions.description: Launched Antin NextGen strategy, a dedicated mid-cap infrastructure platform targeting smaller-ticket investments (EUR 100-500M enterprise value) in next-generation infrastructure themes including digital infrastructure, energy storage, and distributed energy.
actions.stated_rationale: Management identified the mid-cap infrastructure segment as underserved by existing mega-infra funds, with faster deployment pace, lower competition, and strong value-creation potential through operational transformation.
actions.observable_metric_impact: NextGen strategy raised initial capital commitments and deployed across digital infrastructure and energy transition assets. Expanded Antin's addressable investment universe below the flagship fund's typical deal size.
actions.op_prereq_summary: Required dedicated mid-cap investment team with sector specialization, separate fund structure from flagship series, and development of differentiated sourcing networks below mega-cap infrastructure deal flow.

actions.description: Completed IPO on Euronext Paris in September 2021, raising approximately EUR 350M and listing Antin as a publicly traded infrastructure-focused alternative asset manager.
actions.stated_rationale: Management stated the IPO would provide permanent capital for GP commitments, create acquisition currency for potential platform expansion, and increase institutional visibility among global infrastructure LPs.
actions.observable_metric_impact: Raised EUR 350M at IPO. Market capitalization of approximately EUR 4B at listing. Provided balance sheet resources for GP fund commitments and selective strategic investments. Post-IPO share price has fluctuated with fundraising cycle sentiment.
actions.op_prereq_summary: Required partnership-to-corporate conversion, AMF (French regulator) filing and Euronext listing approval, public company governance infrastructure, and investor relations buildout.

actions.description: Expanded deal origination and portfolio management capabilities in North America, opening and scaling a New York office to source infrastructure transactions in the US and Canada alongside the core European franchise.
actions.stated_rationale: Management identified North American infrastructure as a complementary deployment market, particularly in digital infrastructure and energy transition, where Antin's European operational expertise could be applied at scale.
actions.observable_metric_impact: US and North American deal flow contributed to Fund IV and Fund V deployment. Geographic mix shifted from predominantly European to approximately 70% Europe / 30% North America by value.
actions.op_prereq_summary: Required SEC Investment Adviser registration, establishment of New York-based deal origination team, and development of US-focused sector expertise in digital infrastructure and energy markets.

actions.description: Achieved record $7.7B in organic fundraising during 2025 across infrastructure, PE, credit, and public equities strategies, with a 2026 fundraising target of $7B, demonstrating the scalability of Patria's LatAm-focused product suite.
actions.stated_rationale: Management positioned the fundraising momentum as evidence of institutional LP interest in LatAm as a distinctive allocation, arguing that Patria's local origination and operational expertise provide differentiated access to the region.
actions.observable_metric_impact: FEAUM grew to $40.8B (+24% YoY). Pro-forma FEAUM including announced acquisitions reached $47.4B. FRE of $202.5M (+19% YoY). Organic fundraising represented the primary growth driver versus M&A.
actions.op_prereq_summary: Required expanded global investor relations team, track record demonstrating consistent LP returns across multiple fund vintages, and differentiated LatAm macro thesis to attract allocations in a competitive fundraising environment.

actions.description: Expanded distribution into the US institutional market, targeting pension plans, endowments, and insurance companies with Man Group's systematic and solutions strategies. US AUM grew to represent an increasing share of the global base.
actions.stated_rationale: Management identified the US as the largest addressable market for systematic strategies, with significant under-penetration relative to Man Group's established position in European and Asian institutional markets.
actions.observable_metric_impact: Net inflows from US institutional clients contributed to the record $28.7B in net inflows for 2025. US distribution partnerships expanded across consultants and platforms.
actions.op_prereq_summary: Required expanded US-based distribution and consultant relations team, SEC-registered fund vehicles suitable for US institutional mandates, and CFTC/NFA registrations for managed futures strategies.

actions.description: Grew total AUM to $127B (+11% YoY) and fee-earning AUM to $85B by end-2025, driven by successive fundraising across structured capital, private equity secondaries, and real assets strategies beyond the core European direct lending franchise.
actions.stated_rationale: Management described the diversification as reducing dependence on any single strategy or fundraising cycle, with the Amundi partnership providing incremental distribution capacity for newer strategies.
actions.observable_metric_impact: Total AUM $127B (+11% YoY). FEAUM $85B. Q3 fundraising of $4.4B. Non-credit strategies grew as a share of total AUM. Seven distinct strategies now contribute to fee revenue.
actions.op_prereq_summary: Required scaling of non-credit investment teams, development of track records in newer strategies, and leveraging the Amundi distribution partnership for wealth-channel access across multiple product verticals.

actions.description: Achieved record EUR 10.5B in gross inflows during 2025, driven by strong institutional demand across private debt, real assets, and climate strategies. Deployed EUR 7.6B (+35% versus 2024), reflecting improved deployment pace across strategies.
actions.stated_rationale: Management described the fundraising momentum as validation of Tikehau's differentiated positioning as a European mid-market specialist with a balance-sheet-aligned model and growing climate investing franchise.
actions.observable_metric_impact: Total AUM grew to EUR 52.8B (+8% YoY). Fee-paying AUM reached EUR 42B (+6% YoY). EBIT of the AM business grew to EUR 149.6M (+18%). Record deployment of EUR 7.6B indicated strong deal pipeline.
actions.op_prereq_summary: Required expanded global distribution team, maintained LP relationships across European institutional and sovereign wealth investors, and sufficient deal pipeline to support deployment targets.
